title: Injection Techniques | Dermal Filler Chin & Cheek Tutorial

excerpt: Now these lines I find better to treat it 90 degrees so this is the zygomatic muscles causing this it's quite a lot of strength stronger muscles in the face so we're going to do 90 degrees first and then think about The Next Step [Music] foreign [Music] [Music] foreign [Music] [M…

segments.text: now these lines I find better to treat it 90 degrees so this is the zygomatic muscles causing this it's quite a lot of strength stronger muscles in the face so we're going to do 90 degrees first and then think about The Next Step [Music] foreign [Music]
[Music] thank you [Music] head towards midline [Music] foreign [Music]

[Music] foreign [Music] like this is just to help curve it round so that as I'm pushing forwards it's pushing into the hypodermis and not trying to scrape along the underside of the dermis just looking for weak points along the skin that I can use to Target which will create a bit more structure there's a little one there so I might do a little tweak so it's normal as you set up the fat pad will shift slightly forward and this is the one of the key points of resistance

is just anterior to the middle labial ligament I sometimes call this like the Eiffel Tower technique because we've built a tower filler on top here we just need a base for it so I'll put a little bit just a little bit on and it's at the level of the periosteum which is a stronger place to resist then if you're in the hypodermis so [Music] very important not to put too much there because it's obviously widening and it's not feminizing to have a white jaw there just the support I can see it very well from the angle I met there's a little kink in which little dip that's what I'm trying to get rid of

[Music] and I've talked a lot about what I can see now that we've done that and supporting the chin is there is actually a little bit of room for focusing the chin so it's not certainly not an augmentation but just creating more of a heart-shaped at rest if I can put a that make the lower part here the more dominant so just a little bolus here we'll just bring the chin slightly down it's a very small amount in the scope of augmentations it's not an augmentation it's a focusing chin injection midline keep your hand parallel with what you want to produce as I can see here partly because she's

anticipating an injection there's a little bit of tension rotating the chin up this will actually help resist that and we will keep that chin lower down which is the heart shape we're after so I'm going to put a little bit of support here to stop the chin rotating up I'm checking I'm directly in the midline all the way down we enter straight remember a female chin it is often the lower Point that's the lowest point on the face it's not Square so that's why a midpoint entry is good it's created a little bowless and we can go a little each side

and now that we've supported all the structures we're going to do some more superficial work to treat the actual crease using rubella these are very superficial injections in order to unfold this crease now a little test I can see when I pull this apart it doesn't disappear that easily so it may not be a 90 gun hopefully 50 percent and then with time it might get better [Music]
however uncomfortable that was it was worth it foreign [Music]
